---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
plot(cars)
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.

```{r}
#STEP 1: INSTALL AND LOAD REQUIRED PACKAGES
#Run these lines once to install packages (remove # to run):
install.packages("stringr")
install.packages("dplyr")
install.packages("lubridate")
```
```{r}
library(stringr)      # For text manipulation
library(dplyr)        # For data manipulation
library(lubridate)
```
```{r}
# STEP 2: DEFINE THE MAIN SORTING FUNCTION
# ============================================================================

sort_newspaper_articles <- function(input_file,
                                   output_file = "sorted_articles.txt",
                                   separator = "---",
                                   date_pattern = NULL) {
  
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("NEWSPAPER ARTICLE SORTER\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  # Read the text file
  cat("Step 1: Reading file...\n")
  text_lines <- readLines(input_file, warn = FALSE)
  full_text <- paste(text_lines, collapse = "\n")
  cat("✓ File read successfully\n\n")
  
  # Split into individual articles
  cat("Step 2: Splitting articles...\n")
  articles <- str_split(full_text, separator)[[1]]
  articles <- articles[nchar(trimws(articles)) > 0]  # Remove empty ones
  cat("✓ Found", length(articles), "articles\n\n")
  
  # Extract dates from each article
  cat("Step 3: Extracting dates...\n")
  
  # Define date patterns to search for
  if (is.null(date_pattern)) {
    patterns <- c(
      "\\b\\d{1,2}/\\d{1,2}/\\d{4}\\b",          # 1/15/2024 or 01/15/2024
      "\\b\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}\\b",              # 2024-01-15
      "\\b[A-Za-z]+ \\d{1,2}, \\d{4}\\b",       # January 15, 2024
      "\\b\\d{1,2} [A-Za-z]+ \\d{4}\\b",        # 15 January 2024
      "\\b\\d{1,2}-\\d{1,2}-\\d{4}\\b",          # 01-15-2024
      "\\b[A-Za-z]{3,9} \\d{1,2}, \\d{4}\\b",   # Month name variations
      "\\b\\d{1,2}/\\d{1,2}/\\d{2}\\b"           # 1/15/24
    )
  } else {
    patterns <- date_pattern
  }
  
  # Function to find first date in text
  find_date <- function(text) {
    for (pattern in patterns) {
      matches <- str_extract_all(text, pattern)[[1]]
      if (length(matches) > 0) {
        return(matches[1])
      }
    }
    return(NA)
  }
  
  # Extract date from each article
  date_strings <- sapply(articles, find_date)
  cat("✓ Found dates in", sum(!is.na(date_strings)), "articles\n\n")
  
  # Parse dates into Date objects
  cat("Step 4: Parsing dates...\n")
  
  parse_date <- function(date_str) {
    if (is.na(date_str)) return(NA)
    
    # Try multiple parsing approaches
    result <- tryCatch({
      # Try automatic parsing
      parsed <- as.Date(date_str)
      if (!is.na(parsed)) return(parsed)
      
      # Try specific formats
      formats <- c("%m/%d/%Y", "%Y-%m-%d", "%B %d, %Y", 
                   "%d %B %Y", "%m-%d-%Y", "%b %d, %Y",
                   "%d %b %Y", "%m/%d/%y")
      
      for (fmt in formats) {
        parsed <- as.Date(date_str, format = fmt)
        if (!is.na(parsed)) return(parsed)
      }
      
      # Try lubridate functions
      parsed <- mdy(date_str)
      if (!is.na(parsed)) return(parsed)
      
      parsed <- ymd(date_str)
      if (!is.na(parsed)) return(parsed)
      
      parsed <- dmy(date_str)
      if (!is.na(parsed)) return(parsed)
      
      return(NA)
    }, error = function(e) NA)
    
    return(result)
  }
  
  # Parse all dates
  parsed_dates <- sapply(date_strings, parse_date)
  parsed_dates <- as.Date(parsed_dates, origin = "1970-01-01")
  
  successful <- sum(!is.na(parsed_dates))
  cat("✓ Successfully parsed", successful, "dates\n\n")
  
  # Create dataframe with articles and dates
  cat("Step 5: Organizing data...\n")
  article_df <- data.frame(
    article_num = 1:length(articles),
    article_text = articles,
    date_string = date_strings,
    parsed_date = parsed_dates,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Sort by date (articles without dates go to end)
  article_df <- article_df %>%
    arrange(parsed_date, article_num)
  
  cat("✓ Articles sorted chronologically\n\n")
  
  # Show date range
  if (successful > 0) {
    earliest <- min(article_df$parsed_date, na.rm = TRUE)
    latest <- max(article_df$parsed_date, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("Date range: ", as.character(earliest), " to ", as.character(latest), "\n\n")
  }
  
  # Write sorted articles to file
  cat("Step 6: Writing output file...\n")
  
  output_lines <- c()
  output_lines <- c(output_lines, 
                   "========================================",
                   "CHRONOLOGICALLY SORTED NEWSPAPER ARTICLES",
                   paste("Generated:", Sys.time()),
                   paste("Total articles:", nrow(article_df)),
                   paste("Articles with dates:", successful),
                   "========================================",
                   "")
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(article_df)) {
    # Add article header
    if (!is.na(article_df$parsed_date[i])) {
      header <- paste0("\n\n", separator, "\n",
                      "ARTICLE #", i, " | Date: ", article_df$parsed_date[i],
                      "\n", separator, "\n")
    } else {
      header <- paste0("\n\n", separator, "\n",
                      "ARTICLE #", i, " | Date: NOT FOUND",
                      "\n", separator, "\n")
    }
    
    output_lines <- c(output_lines, header, article_df$article_text[i])
  }
  
  # Save to file
  writeLines(output_lines, output_file)
  cat("✓ Output saved to:", output_file, "\n\n")
  
  cat("========================================\n")
  cat("SORTING COMPLETE!\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  # Return the dataframe for further analysis
  return(invisible(article_df))
}
```
```{r}
# STEP 3: HELPER FUNCTION - PREVIEW RESULTS
# ============================================================================

preview_sorted_articles <- function(article_df, n = 5) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("PREVIEW OF SORTED ARTICLES\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  num_to_show <- min(n, nrow(article_df))
  
  for (i in 1:num_to_show) {
    cat("--- Article", i, "---\n")
    
    if (!is.na(article_df$parsed_date[i])) {
      cat("Date:", as.character(article_df$parsed_date[i]), "\n")
    } else {
      cat("Date: NOT FOUND\n")
    }
    
    # Show first 150 characters
    preview_text <- substr(article_df$article_text[i], 1, 150)
    preview_text <- gsub("\n", " ", preview_text)
    cat("Preview:", preview_text, "...\n\n")
  }
}

```
```{r}
# STEP 4: HELPER FUNCTION - ANALYZE RESULTS
# ============================================================================

analyze_results <- function(article_df) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("ANALYSIS REPORT\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  cat("Total articles:", nrow(article_df), "\n")
  cat("Articles with dates:", sum(!is.na(article_df$parsed_date)), "\n")
  cat("Articles without dates:", sum(is.na(article_df$parsed_date)), "\n\n")
  
  if (sum(!is.na(article_df$parsed_date)) > 0) {
    cat("Date Statistics:\n")
    cat("  Earliest:", as.character(min(article_df$parsed_date, na.rm = TRUE)), "\n")
    cat("  Latest:", as.character(max(article_df$parsed_date, na.rm = TRUE)), "\n")
    cat("  Median:", as.character(median(article_df$parsed_date, na.rm = TRUE)), "\n\n")
    
    # Show date formats found
    cat("Date formats found:\n")
    unique_formats <- unique(article_df$date_string[!is.na(article_df$date_string)])
    for (fmt in head(unique_formats, 10)) {
      cat(" ", fmt, "\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Show articles without dates
  no_dates <- article_df %>% filter(is.na(parsed_date))
  if (nrow(no_dates) > 0) {
    cat("\n⚠ Articles without detected dates:\n")
    for (i in 1:min(3, nrow(no_dates))) {
      preview <- substr(no_dates$article_text[i], 1, 100)
      preview <- gsub("\n", " ", preview)
      cat("  Article", no_dates$article_num[i], ":", preview, "...\n")
    }
  }
  
  cat("\n========================================\n\n")
}

```
```{r}
#TUTORIAL STEP 1: Basic Usage
# Run the sorter
result <- sort_newspaper_articles("EMP52_WomansExponenta.txt")
```
